subject: [PATCH v3 2/4] mm: use tiered folio allocation for VM_EXEC readahead

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the readahead window size for executable memory on arm64 page configurations. They explained that contpte requires 2M (32 64K) folios, and updated the exec_folio_order() function to return ilog2(SZ_2M >> PAGE_SHIFT) when PAGE_SIZE is equal to SZ_64K.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara expressed concerns that relying on PMD_SIZE for VMA size estimation may not be suitable, as it could lead to large PMDs (32MB) and inefficient LRU tracking due to the increased likelihood of accessing big folios similarly often. He suggested capping folio size at 2MB until a more sophisticated heuristic is developed.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm not sure relying on PMD_SIZE will be too much for a VMA is a great
strategy. With 16k PAGE_SIZE the PMD would be 32MB large which would fit in
the .text size but already looks a bit too much? Mapping with PMD sized
folios brings some benefits but at the same time it costs because now parts
of VMA that would be never paged in are pulled into memory and also LRU
tracking now happens with this very large granularity making it fairly
inefficient (big folios have much higher chances of getting accessed
similarly often making LRU order mostly random). We are already getting
reports of people with small machines (phones etc.) where the memory
overhead of large folios (in the page cache) is simply too much. So I'd
have a bigger peace of mind if we capped folio size at 2MB for now until we
come with a more sophisticated heuristic of picking sensible folio order
given the machine size. Now I'm not really an MM person so my feeling here
may be just wrong but I wanted to voice this concern from what I can see...

								Honza

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake and agreed to reinstate the previous revision's change in the next patch version.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the feedback! I agree, it makes sense. I did that in the previous
revision [1]. I will reinistante that in the next one.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260320140315.979307-3-usama.arif@linux.dev/

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch updates the Linux kernel to use tiered folio allocation for VM_EXEC readahead, which is used when executable pages are faulted into memory. The goal is to enable hardware TLB coalescing, a technique that reduces the number of page table entries and improves performance. For large VMAs (memory regions), the patch requests HPAGE_PMD_ORDER to map the folio as a PMD-mapped page, which benefits architectures with reasonable PMD sizes. Otherwise, it falls back to exec_folio_order(), which returns the minimum order for hardware PTE coalescing on arm64 and generic architectures. The patch also updates the arm64 exec_folio_order() to return ilog2(SZ_2M >> PAGE_SHIFT) on 64K page configurations, where contpte requires a larger folio size. Additionally, it uses ~__GFP_RECLAIM to make the allocation opportunistic, allowing for smaller folios if a large one is not readily available.
